items.correct: The zigzag movement of sediment along a coast caused by waves approaching at an angle (swash up the beach) and gravity returning water perpendicular (backwash).
items.misconception: Sand moves along the beach because tides pull it sideways.
items.random_wrong: Longshore drift is the rising of sea level over time.
items.why: Students confuse tides with waves. Longshore drift is a wave-driven process — the angle of swash vs straight-down backwash creates the zigzag, not tidal pull.

items.q: How do waves erode a cliff?
items.correct: Through hydraulic action (water compressing air in cracks), abrasion (waves throwing rocks against cliffs), attrition (rocks smashing against each other), and solution.
items.misconception: Waves erode cliffs by pushing water against the rock until it breaks.
items.random_wrong: Cliffs erode because of acid rain dissolving them.
items.why: Water pressure alone is rarely enough. The main mechanisms involve compressed air and rocks acting as tools — students underestimate how erosion needs material to do the wearing.

items.q: Why do meanders migrate across a floodplain?
items.correct: Faster water on the outside of the bend causes erosion (river cliff); slower water on the inside causes deposition (slip-off slope) — over time the channel shifts laterally.
items.misconception: Meanders form because rivers flow around obstacles like rocks.
items.random_wrong: Meanders form because the river is going downhill very steeply.
items.why: Meanders form on gentle gradients, not steep ones, and self-organise rather than dodging obstacles. Students project an intuitive 'water goes around things' model onto a fluid dynamic process.

items.q: How does an OX-BOW LAKE form?
items.correct: A meander loop is cut off when the neck is breached
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A meander loop is cut off when the neck is breached. “Glacier melts in a hollow” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which sequence shows the EROSION of a headland correctly?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cave → arch → stack → stump. “Stack → arch → cave” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Approximately how many METRES tall is High Force waterfall on the River Tees?
items.why: The real figure is 21. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
